assumption_text: The core Misrata episode is bounded to the trapped urban city from 2011-03-06 to 2011-05-15 and excludes later outer-front operations after three front lines were established.
rationale: This aligns the denominator with where civilians were trapped and where the best fighter-series data apply.

assumption_text: Most civilian movement during the siege was internal displacement within Misrata rather than mass exit from the episode theater.
rationale: UN and Amnesty descriptions emphasize trapped civilians and the port as the only evacuation route; reported sea evacuations were limited relative to city population.

assumption_text: McQuinn Figure 4 can be read approximately at several dated chartpoints and interpolated linearly to derive fighter person-months.
rationale: No raw table was published, but Figure 4 is the best source found for Misrata fighter growth over time.

assumption_text: Mixed-status deaths not explicitly classed as civilian or military remain in an unknown-status bucket and are not proportionally allocated in the final DR estimate.
rationale: This avoids forcing ambiguous hospital/committee totals into false precision.

assumption_text: Fighter strength between chart dates changes approximately linearly for person-month purposes.
rationale: Daily values are unavailable; linear interpolation is the least assertive bridge between published points.

assumption_text: Because loyalist attacker exposure was not recovered, the reported DR is explicitly defender-side and marked non-core-eligible.
rationale: Pretending to a full military denominator without attacker deployment data would be more misleading than carrying a partial, labeled ratio.
